$schema_note: Maps release artifacts to their Nexus Mods targets. To publish, create a GitHub Release tagged '<artifact>-v<version>' (e.g. rtc-v3.0.0). The release.yml workflow parses the tag, stages contentDir into installDir, zips it as '<fileBaseName>_v<version>.zip' (so 'bin/...' sits at the zip root), attaches the zip to the release, and uploads to Nexus via Nexus-Mods/upload-action (beta.8, v3 upload API). See RELEASING.md.
$paths_note: contentDir = repo folder whose CONTENTS ship. installDir = path inside the zip those contents are placed at (the in-game install path under the Cyberpunk root). fileBaseName = the download filename stem (version is appended).
$file_id_note: The Nexus file id is NOT stored here. It lives in a REPOSITORY VARIABLE named NEXUS_FILE_ID_<ARTIFACT> (uppercased, '-' -> '_'), set under Settings > Secrets and variables > Actions > Variables. Two reasons: (1) a file_id DOES NOT EXIST until the mod's first file has been uploaded to Nexus BY HAND, so committing a placeholder commits a lie; (2) it is deployment config, not source. It is a VARIABLE and not a secret because it is an identifier, not a credential -- it authorizes nothing without NEXUSMODS_API_KEY, and masking it would only make a wrong id harder to debug. Get it from the mod page's Files tab > 'API Info' (Nexus labels it 'Group ID'). DO NOT use the file_id from the public v1 API -- that is a different id space and the wrong value looks entirely plausible. The workflow still honours a `file_id` key here as a fallback, but new repos should not set one.
